focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Homer is called to perform a circumcision on a baby he delivered, correcting Edna's misunderstanding; Candy and Wally are impressed by his role.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Pregnant young woman seeking an abortion
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Confirms her pregnancy at two months and shows admiration for Homer after a misunderstanding about a procedure.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Awaiting the abortion procedure
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The abortion is yet to be performed
evidence_quotes: CANDY (whispers) Two.
Candy and Wally exchange a worried look.
Candy and Wally can't conceal how impressed they are with the young Homer.

relation_updates: Establishes positive regard for Homer
Reinforces partnership with Wally
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Young woman who whispers her pregnancy confirmation and is impressed by Homer's medical role.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Wally and Homer overhear Candy crying; Homer reassures Wally it's normal post-abortion distress, but Wally, stressed about deployment, grows impatient; they find Candy asleep, her crying ceased.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Candy confronts Wally about aborting their baby because he is leaving for war
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Candy sobs and shouts that she aborted the baby due to fear of Wally not returning from deployment, then rejects his comfort
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Asleep in the backseat after crying uncontrollably
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Fear that Wally may be killed in war and distress over the abortion decision
evidence_quotes: Suddenly Candy starts to sob.
CANDY I couldn't have a baby with someone who's leaving me--I didn't know what else to do!
CANDY I can't have a baby alone. I don't even know if he's coming back!
Wally finds Candy asleep in the backseat.

relation_updates: Physically rejects Wally's comfort
Expresses anger and fear about his deployment
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A woman expressing grief and abandonment fears after an abortion

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Homer talks with Candy about feeling bored and her loneliness; she invites him to see a lobster, then he runs to catch the departing tractor as they part ways, with Candy inviting him back next week.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Candy talking with Homer and inviting him to the lobster pound
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Candy tells Homer she struggles with being alone and invites him to see a lobster at her father's pound
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Homer is invited to return next week
evidence_quotes: When I'm not thinking about Wally. I'm not good at being alone.
You have to come to my dad's lobster pound and see one, then.
Come next week!

relation_updates: Bonds with Homer over shared conversation
Invites Homer to her father's lobster pound
status_updates: Reveals vulnerability about loneliness and思念 for Wally
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Candy, who admits to not being good at being alone

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Homer and Candy playfully chase, then embrace in the pine trees; she initiates a kiss, and they make love, with her guiding him.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Woman initiating sexual intimacy with Homer
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Initiates a kiss and guides Homer in making love, solidifying their physical and emotional connection
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sexually intimate with Homer
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Guilt over relationship with Homer while Wally is at war
evidence_quotes: From a paper bag, Candy is scattering some small, brightly colored pieces of broken glass.
She finds an old piece of glass among the stones and shells at the high-tide mark.
CANDY See? That's last year's glass, or from some year before. I put glass here every year. The ocean makes it beautiful.
Candy holds up a piece of glass to the sun for Homer to look at.

relation_updates: Candy and Homer become sexually intimate, with Candy actively guiding the encounter
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Active agent in her romantic choices

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Homer and Candy argue in a work vehicle about their relationship, torn between their feelings for each other and Candy's history with Wally, leaving them emotionally conflicted.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Candy arguing with Homer about her romantic indecision
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Candy declares love for Homer, acknowledges her history with Wally, and bitterly references their past abortion decision
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Candy drives away after the argument, leaving Homer speechless
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Candy must choose between Homer and Wally and is affected by the abortion decision
evidence_quotes: Do you think I *know* whether I want you or Wally?
I love you, too--I *know* I do.
It's a good thing I didn't have that baby, isn't it?
Her sudden hardness leaves him speechless as they go their separate ways.

relation_updates: Explicitly declares love for Homer
Acknowledges historical bond with Wally as obstacle
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Candy expressing conflict over her relationships with Homer and Wally

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Rose reveals her father, Mr. Rose, is the baby's father, silently confirming Candy's realization, leaving her ashamed and trapped, unable to escape her situation.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Would-be helper to Rose Rose
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Learns through Rose's implication that Mr. Rose is the father of her baby
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Offer to help Rose escape her pregnancy situation
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Realizes Mr. Rose is the father and sees Rose's ashamed expression
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How to help Rose when the father is her own parent
evidence_quotes: Candy slowly gets it. Mr. Rose is the father!
Rose Rose lets that sink in; she keeps looking at Candy with an ashamed expression.
CANDY I know where you can go. Homer and I can take you...
ROSE ROSE I can't go nowhere.

relation_updates: Relationship with Rose becomes strained as Rose rejects her help
View of Mr. Rose shifts to seeing him as perpetrator
status_updates: Gains critical, disturbing knowledge about the baby's paternity
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Concerned friend attempting to assist in a difficult family situation

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Captain Worthington survived 20 days in Burma after his plane crash, following the Irrawaddy River without a compass, now paralyzed waist down with encephalitis B, recovering in Ceylon, due home in four weeks.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Fiancée hearing Major Winslow's report on Wally's condition
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Learns from Major Winslow that Wally is paralyzed from the waist down and will return home in four weeks
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Candy stands and leaves the room after hearing about Wally's paralysis
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Facing Wally's disability and his impending return
evidence_quotes: CANDY Who cares about their *gear*?
Candy stands and leaves the room.

relation_updates: Wally's condition now requires her to confront his disability
status_updates: Wally is paralyzed and due home in four weeks
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Candy, Wally's fiancée, reacting to news of his paralysis

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Homer reacts with sarcasm and detachment as Candy expresses her enduring love for Wally, breaking down in tears while Homer remains emotionally distant and observant.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Lover ending relationship with Homer due to enduring love for Wally
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Declares she never stopped loving Wally and breaks up with Homer, leading to uncontrollable crying
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To be with Wally when he returns
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Crying uncontrollably in the car after the breakup
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The immediate aftermath with Homer in the drive-in theatre
evidence_quotes: Candy grips the steering wheel of the parked car.
Candy covers her face in her hands and cries uncontrollably, unstoppably.

relation_updates: Romantic relationship with Homer ends
Commitment to Wally reaffirmed
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Woman prioritizing love for Wally over relationship with Homer

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Homer declines joining the pickers in moving south, choosing to move on alone as he watches the Worthington house disappear, while Wally shares a tender moment with Candy.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Reunited caregiver
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Candy helps Wally into a wheelchair and expresses intense emotion by sobbing and kissing him repeatedly.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Sobbing and kissing Wally without ceasing while he haltingly touches her face and hair.
evidence_quotes: he sees Olive and Candy and Ray helping Wally out of the car and into a wheelchair.
Candy is sobbing; she kisses Wally, without ceasing, while he haltingly touches her face, her hair.

relation_updates: Reunited with Wally
Begins caregiver role
status_updates: Becomes primary emotional support for Wally
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Caregiving partner

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Candy arrives at the orphanage pregnant and seeking an abortion, then develops a romantic relationship with Homer while her boyfriend Wally is at war. After learning Wally is paralyzed, she ends things with Homer and commits to caring for Wally upon his return.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Larch finds Homer crying and bruised at the second couple's home, confirming rumors they had been abusing him, and carries him away.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Child rescued from abuse by Larch
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Larch bursts into the home, lifts a crying and bruised Homer from his bed, confirms the abuse through rumor, and carries him away.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Homer is removed from the abusive home and returned to St. Cloud's under Larch's care.
evidence_quotes: lifts a crying and bruised Homer out of his bed
The rumor was true. They beat him. He couldn't stop crying.

relation_updates: Larch reinforced as protective caretaker
status_updates: Returned to St. Cloud's under Larch's care
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Orphan whose rescue from abuse establishes a protective bond with Larch.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Homer helps Larch deliver a baby.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Assistant to Larch during delivery
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Homer assists Larch in delivering a baby.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Has assisted in a birth
evidence_quotes: Homer assists Larch in delivering a BABY.

relation_updates: Collaborates hands-on with Larch in deliveries
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Medical apprentice assisting in deliveries

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Homer and Larch debate moral duty in medical practice, with Larch insisting on being of use; they part to work, Homer repeating the phrase as he leaves.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Doctor in moral conflict with mentor
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Homer reaffirms his refusal to perform abortions and repeats Larch's phrase 'of use' to himself as he leaves.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Homer enters an operating room while mumbling 'of use, of use, of use', indicating preoccupation with Larch's advice.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Moral and legal conflict over performing abortions remains unresolved.
evidence_quotes: All I said was, I don't want to perform abortions. I have no argument with you performing them.
In every life, you've got to be of use.

relation_updates: Conflict with Larch over abortion practice reaffirmed
status_updates: Preoccupied with concept of being 'of use'
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Morally principled apprentice who refuses to perform abortions

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Homer leaves with Edna, unable to find Buster; Curly sobs in betrayal as Larch watches the departure, while Buster remains unaware, whittling a stick.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Homer Wells departing St. Cloud's in Wally's car
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Homer says goodbye to children and staff, gets in Wally's car, and leaves St. Cloud's.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Homer is in the passenger seat of Wally's car as it drives away from St. Cloud's.
evidence_quotes: Homer gets in the passenger seat beside Wally
HOMER I have to go, Curly. I'm sorry.
Edna kisses him good-bye.
From the window, Larch watches the car leave.

relation_updates: Wally offers ride and friendship
Edna gives farewell kiss
Curly reacts with betrayal
status_updates: Leaves St. Cloud's in Wally's car
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Homer Wells, an orphan, leaving the only home he has known.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Homer and Candy playfully chase, then embrace in the pine trees; she initiates a kiss, and they make love, with her guiding him.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Romantic partner to Candy
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Homer and Candy become physically intimate on the beach; Candy initiates the kiss and guides him through their first sexual encounter.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: In a physical relationship with Candy while Wally is away
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The relationship is with his friend Wally's girlfriend
evidence_quotes: She is taller than he is, older, obviously more experienced. She initiates the kiss.
They drop to the ground right there; they make love by the roots of the tree, Candy guiding him.
He chases her away from the beach, into the pine trees.
Homer locks his arms around her, from behind. He can't let go.

relation_updates: Intimate relationship established with Candy
status_updates: In a physical relationship with Candy
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Inexperienced young man; Candy is older and more experienced, and she leads the encounter

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Homer offers help to Rose Rose, who is distressed about her pregnancy and feels trapped, but she rejects his support and insists on handling it alone as Mr. Rose calls Homer back to work.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Doctor-like figure assessing and offering help for pregnancy
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Homer examines Rose Rose's pregnancy and suggests a place for help if she doesn't want the baby, but she refuses due to her father's control and insists on handling it alone.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To provide Rose Rose with an option for dealing with her unwanted pregnancy
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Homer's offer is rejected, and he is called away by Mr. Rose, leaving the issue unaddressed.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Rose Rose's dangerous pregnancy situation and her lack of support remain.
evidence_quotes: Homer approaches her bedside; he does so with the authority of a doctor.
ROSE ROSE You think Daddy's gonna let me go anywhere? I ain't going *nowhere*.
MR. ROSE (O.S.) (calling) Homer! Is this a workin' day or what?

relation_updates: Establishes doctor-patient dynamic with Rose Rose
Offer rejected
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Trained medical professional whose assistance is needed but declined

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Homer asserts his role as a doctor while preparing surgical tools, dismisses others but allows Mr. Rose to stay and assist, emphasizing the seriousness of the procedure.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Doctor preparing for an abortion procedure
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Homer scrubs his hands, displays and names surgical instruments, affirms he is a doctor, and allows Mr. Rose to assist.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Homer has prepared the medical equipment and arranged for Mr. Rose's assistance, ready to perform the abortion.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The impending performance of the abortion procedure
evidence_quotes: Homer finished scrubbing his hands in the sink.
HOMER No *almost* about it--I'm a doctor.

relation_updates: Allows Mr. Rose to assist the procedure
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A doctor who asserts and demonstrates his professional role

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Homer learns his X-ray is Fuzzy's, not his own; Dr. Larch falsified it to keep him from going to war, revealing his deep concern for Homer's safety.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Subject of Larch's falsified X-ray deception
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Homer is told by Angela and Edna that his X-ray is actually Fuzzy's and that Dr. Larch lied about his heart condition to keep him from going to war.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Homer is stunned and processes the revelation that his heart is healthy and he was deceived by Larch.
evidence_quotes: EDNA Homer, do you know what this is? HOMER Sure. It's my heart.
ANGELA (shakes her head) Actually, it's Fuzzy's. There's nothing wrong with your heart.
EDNA Dr. Larch wanted to keep you out of the war, Homer--that's why he did it. That's why he told you it was yours.
Homer is stunned; he puts his hand to his heart.

relation_updates: Discovers Larch's protective deception
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Man who believed he had a heart condition, now learns it was fabricated.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Homer reads to the boys, teases tomorrow's story, and leaves with a blessing; the boys close their eyes one by one as darkness returns.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Nighttime reader and blesser for the boys
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Homer reads from 'David Copperfield', teases the next story, and delivers Larch's blessing while dressed in a doctor's coat.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To perform the nightly caretaker routine
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: The boys are settled in semi-darkness with eyes closed after Homer's reading and blessing
evidence_quotes: Homer reads to the boys from "David Copperfield".
Homer, dressed in his long white laboratory coat and looking every inch the doctor, delivers his best imitation of Larch's popular blessing.
HOMER Good night, you Princes of Maine! You Kings of New England!
Homer puts out the lights and leaves the boys in the familiar semi-darkness.

relation_updates: Assumes Larch's paternal role with the boys
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Homer in a white lab coat imitating Larch's blessing

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Homer Wells grows up at St. Cloud's Orphanage under Dr. Larch's mentorship, learning medicine but refusing to perform abortions. He leaves with Wally and Candy, works at Ocean View Orchards, falls in love with Candy, and performs an abortion for Rose Rose at the cider house. After learning his heart condition was fabricated, Homer returns to St. Cloud's and assumes Larch's role as the orphanage doctor.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: A couple enters St. Cloud's orphanage as Larch reflects on broken promises, ignorance, and the value of being useful over successful, admitting he hasn't solved his purpose for coming.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Orphanage director reflecting on his work at St. Cloud's
timeline_nodes.salient_development: In voice-over, Dr. Larch states he has not solved the problem he came to St. Cloud's to address and values being useful over successful.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Dr. Larch remains at St. Cloud's orphanage after reflecting on his unmet objective.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The problem he came to St. Cloud's to solve remains unsolved.
evidence_quotes: LARCH (V.O.) Nor have I solved the problem I came here to solve.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Believes in being useful over being successful

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Larch finds Homer crying and bruised at the second couple's home, confirming rumors they had been abusing him, and carries him away.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Protector rescuing abused child
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Larch bursts into the home, lifts a crying and bruised Homer from his bed, and confirms the abuse rumors with visible rage.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Homer is removed from the abusive home and in Larch's custody.
evidence_quotes: Larch bursts into the home of the second couple
lifts a crying and bruised Homer out of his bed
There is rage in Larch's eyes as he looks at the couple.
LARCH (V.O.) The rumor was true. They beat him. He couldn't stop crying.

relation_updates: Larch intervenes to protect Homer from abuse
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Protector of vulnerable children

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Dr. Larch reflects on his bond with Homer, questioning if his affection made Homer a permanent orphan by making the orphanage his home.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Father figure and medical mentor
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Larch questions if his love has made the orphanage Homer's permanent home.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Homer is involved in the medical examination, and Larch internally reflects on their bond.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Larch's affection has affected Homer's independence.
evidence_quotes: Homer looks adoringly at Dr. Larch
Larch waves Homer over; he places the boy's hand on the woman's abdomen, to feel the fetus kicking.

relation_updates: Larch involves Homer in medical examination, deepening their bond
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Guardian questioning his influence on Homer's attachment

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Homer and Larch debate moral duty in medical practice, with Larch insisting on being of use; they part to work, Homer repeating the phrase as he leaves.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Doctor confronting Homer over refusal to perform abortions
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Larch argues that Homer is obligated to help women seeking abortions, emphasizing the need to be 'of use' in medical practice.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To persuade Homer to accept moral duty over legality and perform abortions
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Larch states his philosophy, but Homer leaves repeating the phrase without agreeing, maintaining his refusal.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Homer's refusal to perform abortions persists
evidence_quotes: LARCH You know how to help these women--how can you not feel obligated to help them when they can't get help anywhere else?
LARCH What else could I have showed you, Homer? The only thing I can teach you is what I know! In every life, you've got to be of use.
HOMER (tiredly; an old topic) All I said was, I don't want to perform abortions. I have no argument with you performing them.

relation_updates: Intensifies conflict with Homer over abortion practice
Establishes adversarial dynamic on this specific issue
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Doctor who insists on being 'of use' in life and medicine

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Homer overhears Larch explaining that records of orphans' mothers are destroyed to help them focus on their futures rather than their pasts.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Orphanage director explaining maternal record destruction to adopting parents
timeline_nodes.salient_development: States policy of destroying maternal records to adopting parents, justifying it as for orphans' future focus
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Convince adopting parents to accept the policy of no maternal records
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Policy disclosed to adopting couple; Homer overhears the explanation
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether destroying maternal records is in the orphans' best interest
evidence_quotes: It is strictly for our orphans' sake that I destroy any record of their natural mothers.
But orphans, especially, should look forward to their *futures*. Not back to their pasts.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: Believes orphans should look forward to their futures, not back to their pasts, by eliminating maternal records.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Larch demands new equipment; Edna announces a delivery. Larch asks Homer to assist, but Homer resists, preferring to fix the film. Larch agrees to deliver while Homer splices, creating tense compromise.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Director of St. Cloud's orphanage, threatened by the Board of Trustees
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Reads a Board letter threatening replacement, argues with Homer over a broken film splice, and negotiates a compromise where he handles a delivery while Homer fixes the film.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To retain his position and obtain new equipment for the orphanage
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Larch agrees to handle the delivery while Homer splices the film, but the Board's threat and tension with Homer persist.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The Board's desire to replace him and ongoing conflict with Homer over responsibilities.
evidence_quotes: LARCH (a harsh whisper) They want to replace me! The Board of Trustees wants to *replace* me!
LARCH Angela, we need a new movie, a new projector, a new typewriter--*that's* what they should replace around here!
HOMER (angry) It's *your* splice! You blame me for everything!
LARCH Sure. Okay. You splice. I'll deliver. It is an uneasy peace.

relation_updates: Tension with Homer over responsibilities
Adversarial stance toward the Board of Trustees
status_updates: Threatened with replacement by the Board
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Defiant leader protecting his institution

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: A girl suffers severe complications from a self-induced abortion with a crochet hook; Larch scolds Homer for inaction as he prepares to operate, lamenting she didn't seek help earlier.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Doctor examining a 12-year-old girl with severe abortion complications and preparing for emergency surgery
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Confronts Homer about his inaction and sedates the girl for surgery
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To sedate the girl and begin surgery to address her injuries
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: The girl is sedated under ether; Homer has left the operating room in anger
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The girl's survival from surgery and the conflict with Homer over responsibility
evidence_quotes: Homer, furious, leaves the operating room.
LARCH I wish you'd come to *me*, dear child. You should have come to me, instead.
The girl is resisting the ether, but her eyelids flutter closed.

relation_updates: Conflict with Homer over responsibility and inaction
status_updates: Performed emergency surgery on a 12-year-old girl
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Doctor focused on immediate medical intervention and direct accountability

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Larch angrily challenges Homer's decision to leave, emphasizing the harsh realities outside; Homer feels trapped but resolute, taking his heart X-ray as a symbol of his past.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Mentor confronting Homer's decision to leave
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Larch angrily questions Homer's choice, expresses doubt about his future, and gives him the heart X-ray.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To persuade Homer not to leave the orphanage
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Homer departs with the heart X-ray, disregarding Larch's opposition
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Larch's concern for Homer's well-being outside and his personal sense of loss
evidence_quotes: LARCH (blows up) Are you really so *stupid* that you imagine you're going to find a more gratifying life?
Larch hands Homer the heart X-ray.
LARCH It's your heart—you ought to take it with you.

relation_updates: Conflict with Homer over departure
Gives Homer heart X-ray
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Angry and protective mentor

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Dr. Larch fabricates credentials and a Harvard diploma for Homer to present him as a qualified doctor, dismissing legal and ethical concerns from Edna and Angela.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Dr. Larch forging credentials to install Homer as his replacement against the Board's plans.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Creates a false biography and forges a Harvard diploma for Homer, presenting him as a qualified doctor to defy the Board.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To secure Homer as his successor and protect St. Cloud's from Board interference.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Possesses forged credentials for Homer but is unaware of his current whereabouts.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The Board's intent to replace him and the illegality of the forged documents.
evidence_quotes: ANGELA Oh, Wilbur, I don't know... (sudden thought) We don't even know where he is!

relation_updates: Protective bond with Homer deepens through deception
Adversarial stance toward Board solidifies
Conspiratorial relationship with Angela and Edna tested
status_updates: Engaged in criminal deception against Board
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Disregards legal and ethical norms to protect St. Cloud's and Homer.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Larch tells Homer he sees him as his work of art and affirms his role as a doctor, emphasizing choice in difficult decisions without interference.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Mentor stating his non-interference policy and defining Homer's role as a doctor
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Larch explicitly calls Homer his 'work of art' and asserts Homer's job is to be a doctor, while explaining he does not interfere in patients' choices between abortion and orphanhood
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To communicate his philosophy and expectations directly to Homer
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Homer has heard Larch declare him as his work of art and affirm his identity as a doctor
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Homer will fulfill being Larch's 'work of art' or simply act as a doctor
evidence_quotes: You are my work of art, Homer. Everything else has been just a job.
I don't know if you've got a work of art in you, but I know what your job is. You're a doctor!

relation_updates: Defines Homer as his masterpiece and successor
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Sees Homer as his 'work of art'

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Angela finds Larch dead, checks his pulse, moves his body, opens a window, and claims the overdose was accidental as Buster joins her.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Dr. Larch's death discovered by Angela
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Angela finds Dr. Larch dead and asserts the overdose was accidental.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Dr. Larch is dead; Angela and Buster are present.
evidence_quotes: Larch is dead.
She pull's Larch's body away from the windowsill.
ANGELA (V.O.) I can assure you that the overdose was entirely accidental.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Homer learns his X-ray is Fuzzy's, not his own; Dr. Larch falsified it to keep him from going to war, revealing his deep concern for Homer's safety.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Disclosure of Dr. Larch's falsified X-ray to protect Homer from war.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Homer learns that Dr. Larch faked his X-ray to keep him out of the war.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Homer is informed of the deception and its protective intent.
evidence_quotes: EDNA Dr. Larch wanted to keep you out of the war, Homer--that's why he did it. That's why he told you it was yours.
ANGELA I think he worried about his own heart. He said it would never stand up to Homer Wells going off to war.

relation_updates: Reinforces fatherly relationship with Homer
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Protective deception to ensure Homer's safety.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Dr. Larch, the director of St. Cloud's orphanage, dedicates his life to caring for orphans, particularly Homer Wells, whom he rescues and mentors. His philosophy of being 'of use' guides his controversial decisions, including destroying maternal records and fabricating credentials for Homer. Facing threats to his position and Homer's departure, he struggles with guilt and attachment, ultimately dying from an ether overdose, leaving a legacy of profound care and ethical ambiguity.
